name: Rodeo a los Picos de Urbión — circular por Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila
region_intro: Road cycling around Molinos de Duero offers diverse terrain within the Comarca de Pinares, characterized by extensive pine forests and the Duero River. The region features prominent natural landmarks such as the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir and the Laguna Negra de Urbión. Cyclists can find a range of routes, from moderate loops around the reservoir to more demanding ascents towards glacial lagoons and mountain passes. Best road cycling routes around Molinos de Duero The most popular road cycling route is Rodeo a los Picos de Urbión — circular por Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila, a 76.1 miles (122.4 km) trail that takes 6 hours 14 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Laguna Negra de Urbión — circular desde Vinuesa, a moderate 23.5 miles (37.8 km) path. This route offers scenic views on a circular path to the Laguna Negra de Urbión from Vinuesa. Local road cyclists also love the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop, a 28.1 miles (45.1 km) trail leading through the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Molinos de Duero is defined by extensive pine forests, the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir, and challenging climbs to features like Laguna Negra. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in Molinos de Duero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 5 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Molinos de Duero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Molinos de Duero?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Molinos de Duero, offering a wide range of options for cyclists of all abilities.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Molinos de Duero?
A: The terrain around Molinos de Duero is diverse, characterized by extensive pine forests of the Comarca de Pinares and the Duero River valley. You'll find routes ranging from moderate loops around the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir to more demanding ascents towards glacial lagoons like Laguna Negra de Urbión and mountain passes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Molinos de Duero?
A: Yes, there are routes suitable for easier rides. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256255342/views-of-the-embalse-de-la-cuerda-del-pozo-round-trip-from-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Embalse de la Cuerda del Pozo — round trip from Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route that offers scenic views without significant elevation gain. In total, there are 3 easy routes available.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: For challenging climbs, consider routes that venture towards the higher elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070846673/rodeo-a-los-picos-de-urbion-circular-por-parque-natural-lagunas-glaciares-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rodeo a los Picos de Urbión — circular por Parque Natural Lagunas Glaciares de Neila&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route with over 2,100 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070363657/santa-ines-and-montenegro-passes-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Inés and Montenegro Passes Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which features significant climbing.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Molinos de Duero?
A: Yes, many routes are designed as loops. A popular moderate circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070911796/cuerda-del-pozo-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering beautiful views of the reservoir. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070381311/laguna-negra-de-urbion-circular-desde-vinuesa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra de Urbión — circular desde Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural features or points of interest can I see while road cycling in the area?
A: You can enjoy diverse natural beauty. Many routes feature the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5572169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, with recreational areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5315870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Pita&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic Laguna Negra de Urbión is a significant natural attraction, and the extensive pine forests of the Comarca de Pinares provide a beautiful backdrop. You might also encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573853" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lost Love Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5585910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza Alta Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is there convenient parking for cyclists in Molinos de Duero?
A: Molinos de Duero, as a historic town and a hub for outdoor activities, offers local amenities. You can typically find parking within the town, which serves as a good starting point for many routes into the surrounding Comarca de Pinares.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Molinos de Duero?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from the serene loops around the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir to the challenging ascents towards Laguna Negra, and the beautiful backdrop of the extensive pine forests.

Q: Are there any towns or villages along the routes where I can find cafes or refreshments?
A: Yes, the nearby town of Vinuesa is a lively hub with numerous shops and cafes, making it a great stop for refreshments. Molinos de Duero itself also offers local amenities. Many routes pass through or near these settlements, providing opportunities for breaks.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Molinos de Duero?
A: The region offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer is also popular, especially for routes that offer shade from the pine forests or opportunities to cool off near the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with other activities in the Molinos de Duero area?
A: Absolutely. The Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir is popular for water sports and has recreational areas like Playa Pita for swimming and sunbathing. The banks of the Duero River also offer excellent conditions for hiking, with approved routes and walkways. The region's natural parks are perfect for nature observation.
tips: The starting point for the Lagunas Glaciares de Neila natural park. The economic activity of this town is logging (sawmills, furniture factories, carpentry workshops, extraction of pine resin, etc.).
Direction to the famous mountain pass not only because it takes you to the foothills of the Lagunas de Neila but also because of its climb that comes from the center of Quintanar de la Sierra.

Beautiful village of Burgos nestled in the hill of the source of the river Neila. From here you can see an impressive view of the surrounding valleys and infinite mountain ridges of the Sierra de la Demanda. It is an ideal place to make a stop to refuel after cycling up.

The Mansilla reservoir is located on the Najerilla river, a tributary of the Ebro river. Its waters hide the old town of Mansilla de la Sierra, whose church and some other buildings are visible when it is low.
Dividing point of the autonomous communities of La Rioja and Castilla León, specifically Soria. Privileged viewpoint of the Sierra de Urbión.
